purpose: To introduce a new physical barrier and set the objective for the next sequence.
summary: Emerging from the forest, the group is confronted by a massive, smooth wall made of white china. The Tin Woodman proposes building a ladder to overcome it.
production_intent: Establish the massive scale of the white china wall as a physical obstacle that halts character progress. The transition from forest exhaustion to bewilderment must be visually anchored by the sudden appearance of the smooth, reflective surface.
scene_short_description: A weary group emerges from a thick forest to face a massive, smooth white china wall.
scene_primary_scale_story_point: The vertical height of the white china wall dwarfs the character group.
scene_start_state: Characters emerge from the dark forest into a clearing at the base of the wall, visibly exhausted.
scene_end_state: The group stands before the barrier, focused on the tin_woodman's proposal to build a ladder.
dominant_action_line: Group discovery of the wall followed by the proposal of a solution.
scene_spatial_layout: Forest edge meets the base of the vertical china structure
Open clearing at the foot of the wall
character_spatial_map: dorothy, the_scarecrow, tin_woodman, cowardly_lion, and toto clustered near the forest exit
beat_transition_map: BT001 -> BT002: visual shock of the barrier
BT002 -> BT003: shift from observation to verbal planning

storyboard_markdown:
# Scene Contract: CH019_SC005

## Production Intent
Establish the massive scale of the white china wall as a physical obstacle that halts character progress. The transition from forest exhaustion to bewilderment must be visually anchored by the sudden appearance of the smooth, reflective surface.

## Required Cast
- dorothy
- the_scarecrow
- tin_woodman
- cowardly_lion
- toto

## Required Environments
- white_china_wall (forest edge/base)

## Continuity Constraints
- Scale relationship between characters and the verticality of the white china wall.
- Texture and smoothness of the white china surface.

## Beat List
- BT001: Discovery of the wall.
- BT002: Reaction to the barrier.
- BT003: The proposal (ladder).
